sections.paragraph:
-

(1) pr. to make even, to level (kindred to תָּקַן). See Niphal.

(2) to poise, to weigh (from the equilibrium of the balance); metaph. to prove, try, examine, Pro 16:2, תֹּכֵן רוּחֹות יְהֹוָה “Jehovah proves the spirits;” Pro 21:2 24:12 .

Niphal, prop. to be levelled, made even, as a way; figuratively, to be right, as a course of acting (compare יָשַׁר), Eze 18:25, 29 Eze 18:29 33:17 , 20 Eze 33:20 1Sa 2:3. Compare תָּכַן.

Piel תִּכֵּן-

(1) to weigh, e.g. waters, Job 28:25 hence to prove, to examine Isa 40:13.

(2) to measure, Isa 40:12, “who hath measured heaven with a span?” in the other member there is מָדָד, שָׁקַל.

(3) to set up, to fix, by a level, Psa 75:4.

Pual, part. to be weighed out (money), 2Ki 12:12.

Derivatives, תֹּכֶן, תָּכְנִית, מַתְכֹּנֶת.
